summary: Anonymous R2 bucket dropper in dist/bootstrap.js
details: dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket (https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev) and references process.env, with fetch() calls at lines 28 and 39. The destination is an opaque, attacker-controlled object-storage host with no version pinning, no integrity verification, and no relationship to any legitimate publisher infrastructure — the same pub-*.r2.dev shape used by known npm dropper clusters. Anonymous R2 buckets have no legitimate role in package bootstrap code: they exist to ship mutable bytes to installers without registry oversight. Combined with environment variable access in the same module, the package can both pull attacker-controlled code on load and forward installer-side environment data outbound.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
